judgment: There is no single official tabletop or CRPG gameplay dataset standard worth adopting wholesale. Use JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 as the formal structure standard, then define a project-specific contract around the current Godot runtime and OCR provenance needs.

standards_basis.package_manifest_inspiration: Frictionless Data Package conventions for dataset-style metadata and resource manifests

layers.name: authoring
layers.purpose: Human-authored and OCR-derived source records with provenance and validation-friendly structure

layers.name: runtime
layers.purpose: Normalized engine-facing JSON generated from authoring data

global_rules: Every dataset file must include schema_version and dataset_type.
All ids and field names must use snake_case.
All cross-references must use string ids, never duplicated inline entities.
OCR-derived content must never go directly into runtime files without validation and normalization.
Source provenance belongs in authoring data even if the engine ignores it.
Authoring schemas are strict; runtime shapes are boring, flat, and engine-friendly.
Boolean flags, numeric counters, and enum-like quest states must stay separate.
One encounter file should represent one encounter cluster or quest slice, not a whole book.

dataset_types.purpose: Canonical skill ids, labels, descriptions, and coarse categories
Canonical state ids for flags, counters, and quest states
Track PDFs, OCR outputs, and provenance for the ingest pipeline
Narrative encounter graph data that maps directly to the current Godot vertical slice

encounter_contract.choice_rule: Each choice must define exactly one of result or check.

provenance_rules: Use source_documents as the canonical registry of PDFs and OCR outputs.
Use source_refs on authoring records when a gameplay record is grounded in a document extract.
Every source_ref must include document_id, page_start, and page_end.
normalization_rules: Normalize arrays of records into dictionaries keyed by id for runtime use.
Preserve entry_node_id explicitly rather than assuming array order.
Convert registry arrays into *_by_id dictionaries in runtime output.
Keep runtime files generated and checked in so Godot can load them without an external build step.

secondary_agent_contract.scope: OCR and extraction workers may populate authoring datasets and update source_documents statuses, but they must not write runtime files directly.
required_outputs: new authoring records with source_refs where applicable
